channel.title: NYT > U.S. > Politics
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: World Leaders Push to Save Iran Talks Amid Israel’s Attacks in Lebanon
item.description: President Trump’s cease-fire with Iran appears at risk as Vice President JD Vance heads to Pakistan for discussions with Iranian officials.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A Lebanese man stood on a pile of debris in the Corniche El Mazraa area of Beirut after the Israeli bombardment of the capital on Wednesday.

item.title: F.A.A. Says Military Can Use Anti-Drone Lasers in U.S. Airspace
item.description: An interagency dispute over the use of high-energy lasers near airports led the Federal Aviation Administration to temporarily close the airspace twice in Texas this year.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.category: Laser (Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation)
item.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Locust, an anti-drone laser system that has been used recently along the border with Mexico, last month in Albuquerque, N.M.

item.title: Iran Unable to Find Mines It Planted in Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Says
item.description: The lost mines have prevented Iran from quickly complying with President Trump’s demand to allow more ships to pass through the waterway.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo ships near the Strait of Hormuz, as seen last month from northern Ras al-Khaimah in the United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Trump Moves Top Aide James Blair to Political Operation Ahead of Midterms
item.description: Mr. Blair will “lead the charge from the outside,” the president said on social media. The move comes as Republicans are bracing for a potentially difficult November election.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:description: James Blair, a deputy chief of staff, is taking over President Trump’s outside political operation and its $300 million-plus budget, Mr. Trump announced on Friday.

item.title: Eric Swalwell Faces Sex Assault Accusation as He Runs for California Governor
item.description: Eric Swalwell, a Democratic congressman, denied the accounts published by CNN and The San Francisco Chronicle. Nancy Pelosi was among those who said he should withdraw from the California governor’s race.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall and Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Campaign officials and supporters are abandoning Representative Eric Swalwell.

item.title: Possible Democratic 2028 Candidates Are Already Fighting for Black Voters’ Support
item.description: Possible presidential candidates are already courting a crucial Democratic constituency.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.media:description: Political activists watch former Vice President Kamala Harris speak during the National Action Network convention in Manhattan on Friday.

item.title: Eliot Engel, a 16-Term Bronx Liberal in Congress, Dies at 79
item.description: After defeating a Democratic fixture in a 1988 primary, he rose to lead the House Foreign Affairs Committee, then was toppled himself in 2020, undone by an overheard comment.
item.dc:creator: Sam Roberts
item.media:description: Representative Eliot Engel in his Bronx office in 1998. He was a reliable champion of liberal causes.

item.title: Trump Says First Lady ‘Had a Right’ to Talk About Epstein
item.description: President Trump said in an interview that he had known his wife wanted to address rumors about the late sex offender at some point, but that he had not known exactly what she would say.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.media:description: Melania Trump before speaking in the Grand Foyer of the White House on Thursday.

item.title: Potential Democratic 2028 Candidates Condemn Trump’s ‘War of Choice’ in Iran
item.description: At a convention in New York, several Democrats mulling presidential bids rallied around opposition to President Trump’s decision to attack Iran.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Burned Before, Democrats Abruptly Revive the Idea of Impeaching Trump
item.description: Democrats in Congress have assiduously avoided talk of a third impeachment of President Trump, concerned that it would distract from their midterm campaign message. That tide seems to have turned.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump returning to the White House last month.

item.title: Homeland Security Workers Get Paid, but the Next Check Is Uncertain
item.description: The lack of clarity has frustrated employees as Congress battles over how to fund the department.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo and Michael Gold
item.media:description: A security line at Pittsburgh International Airport. Before President Trump ordered pay for T.S.A. officers last month, many were calling in absent at major airports.

item.title: Officials Release Design for 250-Foot Arch in Washington, as Trump Seeks Another Imprint
item.description: The president has proposed the arch, which would rise on a Washington roundabout across from the Lincoln Memorial, as a way to celebrate America’s 250th anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.media:description: A rendering released by the U.S. Commission of Fine Arts of plans for a 250-foot triumphal arch in Washington.

item.title: Democrats Denounce Trump’s Plan to Use Foreign Steel in White House Ballroom
item.description: A New York Times report that the president was accepting a donation of foreign steel for the ballroom was seized on by Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Elizabeth Warren, Democrat of Massachusetts, said in a letter that the move “casts doubt on the president’s commitment to supporting the American steel industry.”

item.title: ‘I’m Fed Up.’ Frustrated With Trump, Starmer Embraces Other Allies.
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain is strengthening ties in Europe and the Middle East as the once-special relationship with America sours.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain boarding an airplane, in Doha, Qatar, on Friday, after a three-day trip to the Gulf.

item.title: Judge Declines to Order Trump Administration to Adjust Somali Deportation Policies
item.description: Acknowledging that the government may have recently stepped up the pace of removal proceedings, a federal judge wrote that he could not immediately intervene.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Border Patrol agents in Minneapolis in January, after questioning employees of a Somali American-owned business about their immigration status.

item.title: Trump Posts Graphic Video of Deadly Attack and Rails Against Haitian Immigrants
item.description: The man seen beating a woman is from Haiti and faces a murder charge in Florida, officials said. President Trump has fought to end protections for Haitian immigrants.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon and Jacey Fortin
item.media:credit: Tom Brenner for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Monday. Mr. Trump has made disparaging comments about Haitian immigrants for years.

item.title: European Airports Warn of Jet Fuel Shortages if Strait of Hormuz Remains Shut
item.description: An association of airports told European Union officials that fuel shipments through the Strait of Hormuz had to restart within three weeks to avoid a “systemic” shortage.
item.dc:creator: Niraj Chokshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Travelers at Frankfurt Airport in Germany on Friday.

item.title: Iran War Drives Deeper Oil Shock Than Prices Reveal
item.description: The war with Iran is preventing huge amounts of oil from flowing out of the Persian Gulf, but the prices that many people track don’t fully capture the scale of the disruption.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

item.title: Vance Faces Test of His Negotiating Skills With Iran Talks
item.description: Vice President JD Vance is leading negotiations this weekend toward an end to a war that he had opposed starting.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Elizabeth Frantz for The New York Times
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance is traveling to Pakistan at the end of the week to hold talks with the Iranians.

item.title: To Fill Air Traffic Controller Shortage, F.A.A. Turns to Gamers
item.description: Avid players of video games have emerged as a target demographic for recruiters at a range of federal agencies, including the military and the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.media:credit: Kent Nishimura for The New York Times
item.media:description: The air traffic control tower at Ronald Reagan National Airport.

item.title: Harris Says She Is ‘Thinking About’ Running for President Again in 2028
item.description: At a convention in New York, possible candidates for the Democratic presidential primary are making overtures to Black voters, a vital constituency.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck and Tim Balk
item.media:description: Kamala Harris, former vice president and the 2024 Democratic presidential candidate, spoke at the National Action Network Convention in New York on Friday.

item.title: TSA Lines Are Shorter. The World Cup and a Lengthy Shutdown Could Change That.
item.description: Security lines are shorter, but the shutdown continues and pay is unresolved. With the World Cup around the corner, T.S.A. agents are tempering their expectations.
item.dc:creator: Ceylan Yeğinsu
item.media:description: Back pay for Transportation Security Administration agents has recently been restored, but questions remain about future paychecks as the partial government shutdown continues. Above, T.S.A. employees at Ronald Reagan National Airport in the Washington area on March 30.

item.title: Trump’s Changes Lock Some Employers Out of H-1B Visa Program
item.description: Since imposing a $100,000 fee on new visas in September, the Trump administration has upended the skilled worker program.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sara McCabe, the president of the Wayside Youth & Family Support Network, a nonprofit that has struggled in recent years to recruit teachers for its special education school.
